mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in unemployment rate above 9.5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce corporate profit visibility, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 9.5%, or the signal's IC < 0.02 in rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in industrial production and housing starts indicate weak supply-side activity, creating deflationary pressures that drive positive S&P 500 forward returns through improved real rate expectations over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to produce positive returns in > 50% of cases, or the interaction term's coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample regressions.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in real sales (RSAFS) combined with slowing GDP growth (GDPC1) signals weak aggregate demand, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns during late-cycle economic deceleration periods.
failure_condition: The interaction signal fails to predict negative returns in > 50% of cases, or the individual proxies show no significant correlation with SPX returns in the same period.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) above 100k in a month signals robust labor market conditions that boost consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 100k, or the signal's IC < 0.02 in rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in CPI inflation below 1.5% signals deflationary risks that prompt central bank intervention, creating positive S&P 500 forward returns through liquidity injections over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL falls below 1.5%, or the signal's IC < 0.02 in rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in housing starts (HOUST) following a 3-month decline indicates improving demand conditions, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days during recovery phases.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 50% of instances where HOUST rebounds > 5% from 3mo low, or the signal's IC < 0.03 in rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production (INDPRO) above 95 suggests expanding supply capacity that reduces inflationary pressures, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns through lower real rates over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 50% of instances where INDPRO exceeds 95, or the signal's IC < 0.02 in rolling 1-year windows.
